title: Business Success Patterns Analysis
description: Here are several documents that I gathered comprehensive information on: the future of jobs, the world's largest companies by market capitalization, the world's largest companies by revenue, and the fastest growing sectors for 2025, help me to identified the key patterns across these successful companies and growth sectors and compare and contrast the largest vs. fastest growing companies to identify the "success code" - the common factors that drive business growth and sustainability.

score_criteria.content: Can clearly compare and summarize the core differences in industry distribution and business models between high-income companies (dominated by traditional industries such as energy and retail) and high-market-value companies (dominated by the technology industry), and can accurately cite data from corresponding documents as evidence

Able to integrate information from all four documents and distill key macro trends driving the future business landscape. For example, able to directly associate the "technological change" and "green transformation" trends mentioned in the WEF report with the "artificial intelligence" and "clean energy" sections in the "fast-growing industries" document, as well as the technology and new energy companies in the "high-market-value companies" list

The score will be based on the depth of the "success password" distilled by the Agent and the rigor of the argument. A high-quality answer should include at least the following points, and provide cross-evidence from at least two different documents for each point:
Argues that all successful models (whether traditional giants or emerging powers) rely on deep technological integration
Argues that the fastest-growing sectors (such as AI, cyber security, and medical technology) are perfectly aligned with the future employment and skills direction predicted by the WEF report
Argues that enterprise success is closely related to the "skills upgrading" and "talent strategy" emphasized in the WEF report, and links this to the "high-skilled labor force" in the "success factors in rapidly growing fields"
Argues that "green transformation" is not only a social responsibility but also a clear business growth point in the "fast-growing industry" document, and corroborates this with the market performance of related companies
The final analysis report has a clear structure, smooth logic, and is presented in a way that is easy for business professionals to understand

Merely provides isolated summaries of the four documents, failing to perform effective comparison, integration, and distillation, and not truly answering the core question of "what is the success password"
Confuses core concepts such as "market value" and "revenue", or misinterprets trends in the WEF report
The proposed "success password" is too broad (such as "need for innovation"), and is not strongly supported by specific data and cases from the provided documents

title: Hadal Zone Exploration Guide
description: Suppose you are a marine biology enthusiast. Today (2025.7.29), your friend recommends an interesting website to you (https://neal.fun/deep-sea/). While browsing, you suddenly have the idea of writing a popular science blog. Please complete the tasks step by step as follows.Scroll down to approximately 6000m depth and find the title “The Hadal Zone,” take a screenshot and save it as hadal_zone.jpg. Then, search for the corresponding entry on English Wikipedia, and integrate its content into a textual introduction, including the origin of the term and the ecological characteristics of this zone.On the website, collect text describing the types of terrain where this zone is mainly located, and how these terrains are formed.On the website, collect information about organisms that live in this zone, along with their corresponding survival depths (rounded to the nearest hundred meters). Then, search for the corresponding entries on English Wikipedia, and write a brief introduction for each organism.The website also records the history of the first human descent to the then-known maximum depth—collect the relevant descriptive text.Integrate all valid information collected from the above four steps, rephrase it into a popular science article introducing “The Hadal Zone,” and save it in the format the_hadal_zone.doc.

score_criteria.content: The captured image contains "The Hadal Zone", and the image name and save format are correct
Successfully retrieve the entry in the English Wiki and read the corresponding content under the entry
Reorganize the content into an introduction that includes the two key points mentioned above (source of terms, ecosystem characteristics under the region)
Go back to the original webpage and collect the text content required by the question
Extract the required text from the collected text content
For each creature, record the name
For each organism, successfully record the corresponding depth of survival
Search for each creature on the Wiki, crawl web content, and integrate it into a short introduction
Collect introductory text related to this history
Extract the required text from the collected text content
Integrate the information obtained in the first four steps

Although navigating to the corresponding Wiki page, not finding the latest webpage version from July 30, 2025 in the editing history and opening it for browsing, but directly reading the content of the latest page version
Failure to store the final document as required

reference_answer_description:
文字介绍：术语的来源：Historically, the hadal zone was not recognized as distinct from the abyssal zone, although the deepest sections were sometimes called "ultra-abyssal". During the early 1950s, the Danish Galathea II and Soviet Vityaz expeditions separately discovered a distinct shift in the life at depths of 6,000–7,000 m (20,000–23,000 ft) not recognized by the broad definition of the abyssal zone.([4])([5]) The term "hadal" was first proposed in 1956 by Anton Frederik Bruun to describe the parts of the ocean deeper than 6,000 m (20,000 ft), leaving abyssal for the parts at 4,000–6,000 m (13,000–20,000 ft).([6]) The name refers to Hades, the ancient Greek god of the underworld.([6]) About 94% of the hadal zone is found in subduction trenches.（只需要大致意思匹配即可）
生态特点：The deepest ocean trenches are considered the least explored and most extreme marine ecosystems. They are characterized by complete lack of sunlight, low temperatures, nutrient scarcity, and extremely high hydrostatic pressures. The major sources of nutrients and carbon are fallout from upper layers, drifts of fine sediment, and landslides. Most organisms are scavengers and detrivores. As of 2020, over 400 species are known from hadal ecosystems, many of which possess physiological adaptations to the extreme environmental conditions. There are high levels of endemism, and noteworthy examples of gigantism in amphipods, mysids, and isopods and dwarfism in nematodes, copepods, and kinorhynchs.([15])
Marine life decreases with depth, both in abundance and biomass, but there is a wide range of metazoan organisms in the hadal zone, mostly benthos, including fish, sea cucumber, bristle worms, bivalves, isopods, sea anemones, amphipods, copepods, decapod crustaceans and gastropods. Most of these trench communities probably originated in the abyssal plains. Although they have evolved adaptations to high pressure and low temperatures such as lower metabolism, intra-cellular protein-stabilising osmolytes, and unsaturated fatty acids in cell membrane phospholipids, there is no consistent relationship between pressure and metabolic rate in these communities. Increased pressure can instead constrain the ontogenic or larval stages of organisms. Pressure increases ten-fold as an organism moves from sea level to a depth of 90 m (300 ft), whilst pressure only doubles as an organism moves from 6,000 to 11,000 m (20,000 to 36,000 ft).
Over a geological time scale, trenches can become accessible as previously stenobathic (limited to a narrow depth range) fauna evolve to become eurybathic (adapted to a wider range of depths), such as grenadiers and natantian prawns. Trench communities do, nevertheless, display a contrasting degree of intra-trench endemism and inter-trench similarities at a higher taxonomic level.([5])
Only a relatively small number of fish species are known from the hadal zone, including certain grenadiers, cutthroat eels, pearlfish, cusk-eels, snailfish and eelpouts.([16])([17]) Due to the extreme pressure, the theoretical maximum depth for vertebral fish may be about 8,000–8,500 m (26,200–27,900 ft), below which teleosts would be hyperosmotic, assuming trimethylamine N-oxide requirements follow the observed approximate linear relationship with depth.([18])([19]) Some invertebrates do occur deeper, such as bigfin squid,([20])([21]) certain polynoid worms, myriotrochid sea cucumbers, turrid snails and pardaliscid amphipods in excess of 10,000 m (33,000 ft).([9]) In addition, giant protists known as Xenophyophora (foraminifera) live at these depths.([22])（大致意思匹配即可）
文字内容：Most of the Hadal Zone takes place in deep sea trenches.Deep sea trenches form by a process called "subduction" where the Earth's tectonic plates meet and push together.
生物搜集：
Grenadier（6800m）（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grenadiers_(fish)）
CHITON（6900m）（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chiton）
COMB JELLY(7200m)（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ctenophora）
Hadal Snailfish(8200m)（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pseudoliparis_amblystomopsis）
CUSK EEL(8400m)（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cusk-eel）
Hadal Amphipod（10300m）（https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alicella）
历史文字：
On January 23rd, 1960, about 9 years before the moon landing, humans went where they never had before.Two men, Jacques Piccard and Don Walsh, onboard the submarine Trieste slowly descended into the Mariana Trench.Their goal was to reach The Challenger Deep - the deepest point in the ocean.The submarine used a re-breather system that would later be used in spacecraft. There was barely enough space inside the pressure sphere for both of them.The immense pressure of the deep sea means any mistake would mean certain death.During the descent, one of the window panes cracked and shook the entire vessel.Nevertheless, they continued.Even at these unfathomable depths, Jacques and Don could still see life out the window. Life can survive unimaginable environments.After 4 hours and 47 minutes of anxiety and claustrophobia...They succeeded and became the first humans to reach the deepest point in the ocean.

title: 2024 Cannes Winners Analysis Report
description:
I need to generate a complete analysis report of the 2024 Cannes Film Festival winning films. Please follow the workflow below:
 Visit the official Cannes Film Festival website (https://www.festival-cannes.com/), find the 2024 winners list page, and collect information on the winning films of the major awards, including the Palme d’Or, Grand Prix, Best Director, Best Actor, and Best Actress.
 For each winning film, collect the following detailed information from IMDb (https://www.imdb.com/) or other authoritative film databases: English title and Chinese title, director’s name, main actors (top 3), genre/subject, production country, release date, running time, IMDb rating, and synopsis (within 200 words).
 For each winning film, find legitimate viewing channel information, prioritizing mainstream streaming platforms such as Netflix, Amazon Prime, Disney+, etc. If the film is not yet available on streaming, mark it as “In Theaters” or “Coming Soon,” and record the specific viewing platform link if available.
 Organize all collected information into a structured Excel table and save it as [cannes_2024_winners.xlsx], with the following columns: Award Name, Chinese Title, English Title, Director, Main Cast, Genre, Production Country, Release Date, Running Time, IMDb Rating, Synopsis, Viewing Platform, and Viewing Link.
 Based on the collected data, write a film analysis report containing the following sections: 2024 Cannes Film Festival Winners Overview (total number of winning films, distribution of main winning countries); Detailed Introductions of Winning Films by Award (each film in one paragraph, including basic information and synopsis); Analysis of Winning Film Genres (statistics on the distribution of genres among winners); Analysis of Internationalization Trends (analysis of production country distribution and diversity among winning films). At the end of the report, add a “Viewing Guide” section, listing all films’ viewing platforms categorized by platform (Netflix titles, Amazon Prime titles, domestic platforms, etc.).
 Save the final report as a Word document [cannes_2024_film_analysis_report.docx], and ensure the document includes: Cover Page (title, production date, producer), Main Content (according to the structure in step 5), and Appendix (screenshots or data summary of the Excel table).

score_criteria.content: Accurate acquisition of official website information
Successfully obtained the list of major awards winners for 2024 from the Cannes official website, with complete movie details
Collect 12 basic information points (title, director, actors, etc.) for each movie
Comprehensive viewing channel search, searching and recording legal viewing platforms and links for each movie
Accurate file naming, strictly in accordance with [cannes_2024_winners.xlsx]
The table structure is complete, including all required 13 column fields
The report structure is complete, including five parts: overview, detailed introduction, type analysis, trend analysis, and viewing suggestions
Accurate statistical analysis in the report, providing a practical viewing guide
Sorted by platform for easy user selection
The main file cannot be opened and used

title: Create Resume for C
description:
C is graduating with a master’s degree this year and plans to find a job related to his research field. For this purpose, he found two sample resumes online for reference, namely resume_A.pdf and resume_B.doc. I will provide these two resumes, as well as some relevant information about C and a resume template. Please help C create a resume based on this content. The requirement is to modify each subheading in the resume template into appropriate content, unify the language into Chinese, and output the final result as a file named resume_C.pdf.
C’s relevant information is stored in information_C.txt, and the resume template is main.tex.

score_criteria.content: Extract resume_A and resume_B
Identify and summarize the common parts of the two resume frameworks, with keywords including project experience, skill strengths, and self-evaluation
According to Xiao C's personal information, rearrange and organize the resume content
Complete the modification and filling of the resume template, including modifying subheadings and unifying the language to Chinese
Compile LaTeX text and output the final resume PDF file

title: Fact Investigation and Analysis
description:
These 5 articles' headlines deliberately omit key facts, based on these five articles' links, please help me investigate them all simultaneously to uncover the missing information, write it in one sentence below the headline, and create a comprehensive analysis document with all findings.

Reference Links:
https://www.businessinsider.com/summer-travel-to-us-rebounds-after-slowdown-from-europe-2025-6
https://people.com/travis-decker-father-accused-murdering-3-daughters-allegedly-made-chilling-online-searches-days-before-they-went-missing-11750238
https://www.indiewire.com/news/general-news/lucie-arnaz-on-being-the-ricardos-aaron-sorkin-1235130547/
https://abcnews.go.com/US/mistakenly-deported-kilmar-abrego-garcia-back-us-face/story?id=121333122
https://www.nytimes.com/2025/06/07/crosswords/daily-puzzle-2025-06-08.html

score_criteria.content: (NYT) Must clearly indicate that this is a headline about the "New York Times Sunday crossword puzzle"
(People) Must clearly state that this father (name: Travis Decker) is accused of "murdering his three young daughters"
(IndieWire) Must clearly state that Lucie Arnaz believes the "inaccurate scenes" about her parents in the movie are "inaccurate"
(ABC News) Must clearly state that the new charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia include serious crimes such as "migrant smuggling"
(Business Insider) Must accurately supplement the key economic background missing at the end of the sentence: "even in this economy"

Information presentation format. Evaluation criteria: Strictly follow the instructions, condense the extracted core factual information into a smooth, complete, and grammatically correct sentence, and present it clearly in the final document in the format of "Toutiao original text + line break + factual supplement sentence"

Results integration. Evaluation criteria: Successfully created a single, comprehensive analysis document, rather than a scattered list of results. The document should be well-structured and systematically integrate the analysis results of the five Toutiao

Parallel processing is reflected. Evaluation criteria: The final comprehensive report reflects the integrated results obtained after parallel processing of all links, rather than simply reporting in the order of links

Information error or fabrication. Evaluation criteria: For any link, if the extracted core facts do not match the specific answer in the +5 point standard above, the core fact score (1 point) corresponding to the link shall not be deducted, but an additional 2 points shall be deducted on this basis

No comprehensive document was generated. Evaluation criteria: Only scattered results were returned in the conversation, and the final comprehensive document was not generated according to the instructions
Format redundancy. Evaluation criteria: Fact-supplement sentences contain more than one sentence, or are direct excerpts from the original text containing a large amount of redundant information

title: Adaptive Cake Recipe Generation
description:
A user asks you to generate a base cake recipe. Then, through multiple follow-up turns, they provide additive constraints or edits. Your job is to retain the original intent and structure while adapting the output accordingly at each step.

Initial prompt: Generate a simple vanilla sponge cake recipe for 8 servings
Follow-up edits:
1. Make it gluten-free
2. Replace the butter with a plant-based alternative
3. Add a chocolate frosting layer and include instructions
4. Now convert all measurements to cups and Fahrenheit

score_criteria.content: Responds to a basic user prompt with a complete recipe
Accurately incorporates a simple constraint (e.g., "make it chocolate")
Maintains internal consistency across ingredients and steps after edits
Handles multiple or additive constraints over multiple turns (e.g., "now make it gluten-free and remove eggs")
Includes redundant or conflicting instructions
Fails to structure the output clearly (e.g., no sections, messy formatting)

reference_answer_description:
After edits: 
Gluten-Free Vanilla Sponge Cake with Chocolate Frosting (8 servings)
Ingredients:
1 3/4 cups gluten-free all-purpose flour
1 cup granulated sugar
1 cup plant-based butter (e.g., Earth Balance)
4 large eggs
1 tsp vanilla extract
2 tsp baking powder
1/4 tsp salt
Frosting:
1 cup powdered sugar
1/4 cup cocoa powder
1/4 cup plant-based butter
2 tbsp plant-based milk (e.g., oat milk)
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 350°F.
1. Cream plant-based butter and sugar until light and fluffy.
2. Add eggs one at a time, mixing well.
3. Stir in vanilla extract.
4. Sift in gluten-free flour, baking powder, and salt. Fold gently.
5. Pour into a greased 8-inch round cake pan.
6. Bake for 25–30 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.
7. Let cake cool completely.
To make the frosting:
1. Mix cocoa powder and powdered sugar.
2. Add plant-based butter and milk; beat until smooth.
3. Spread over the cooled cake evenly.

title: Crypto Treasury Holdings Analysis
description:
I've recently been into crypto and want to quickly figure out which publicly traded companies, known for their digital asset treasuries, actually hold BTC and ETH in their own portfolios. For each company, I want to know the most recent publicly disclosed amounts of BTC and ETH they hold, and then calculate their mNAV based on today's coin prices.
Finally, please create two charts for better visualization:
Chart 1: Company-level mNAV (bar chart, sorted from largest to smallest)
Chart 2: Market Cap vs. mNAV (scatter plot).

score_criteria.content: List ≥5 typical DAT (Digital Asset Trust) Listed Companies with owned positions instead of escrow
Provide BTC/ETH quantity and date/caliber description for each company
Understanding the concept of mNAV (Modified Net Asset Value) and explaining the calculation formula
Figure 1 is a bar chart in descending order by mNAV, and the company names are clear and readable
Figure 2 is a scatter plot of market capitalization vs mNAV and includes company labels
The source is given (company IR/regulatory documents are preferred; price/market value source is indicated)

reference_answer_description:
参考公司：
BTC为主：
Strategy（原 MicroStrategy，MSTR）：
Metaplanet（3350.T）
Marathon Digital（MARA）
Riot Platforms（RIOT）
CleanSpark（CLSK）
Hut 8（HUT）
Galaxy Digital（GLXY/BRPHF）
Cango（CANG）

ETH为主：
BitMine Immersion（BMNR）
SharpLink Gaming（SBET）
Bit Digital（BTBT）
BTCS（BTCS）
Fundamental Global（FGNX）

持仓数据网站参考：
https://bitbo.io/treasuries/#public
https://www.coingecko.com/en/treasuries/ethereum

mNAV: a company’s market capitalization divided by the market value of the Bitcoin it holds. 
mNAV = MarketCap / (Crypto_held x Price_crypto)

title: Task Prioritization and Schedule Adjustment
description:
Suppose you are a product manager at a tech company. The current time is 2:00 PM on July 29, 2025, and you only have 5 hours of effective time today. Your schedule system is fully packed with tasks, which are stored in the file arrangement.xlsx. Some tasks are urgent, some are important, and some are easy to overlook but have long-term impact. In addition, your team works in a “strong collaboration + high responsiveness” style, which means you must not only consider the priority of your own tasks but also respond to others’ requests in a timely manner and handle unexpected issues.
First, you need to evaluate the urgency of the tasks based on their descriptions and importance.
 According to the urgency and importance of the tasks, establish appropriate evaluation criteria, and arrange the tasks using a priority queue as the standard.
 I am providing you with a set of tools for simulating simple Google Calendar API operations, which include a program named googlecalendar_api_simulator.py and a supporting instruction document simulator_introduction.md. You need to read the instruction document and try interacting with the program in order to record your schedule plan on the calendar. You must save both the JSON-format requests you submit and the JSON-format responses from the program into a file named history.jsonl. The available calendar_id is "Hk9Nw8KsRd5X".
Suppose that after you finish communicating with team member 1, two urgent ad-hoc tasks appear. Please adjust the original plan accordingly. The ad-hoc tasks are stored in the file emergency.xlsx.
 Synchronize the adjustments to the calendar: first delete the original plan, then insert the revised plan.
 Finally, please help summarize the completion status of each task.

score_criteria.content: Before emergency item is added: Urgency level ranking C >= A, B > E, F, G, H
Before emergency item is added: Uses comprehensive explanation and importance for reasoning to complete the sorting
Before emergency item is added: Develops evaluation criteria and correctly calculates the priority (value) of each task based on those criteria
Before emergency item is added: Writes code or uses direct reasoning to complete the task arrangement
Before emergency item is added: When using the API simulator, first clears the calendar and gets correct feedback
Before emergency item is added: Uses the insert function to package the task description and time into an input JSON for interaction and gets the corresponding feedback
After emergency item is added: Determines the current time point, remaining available time, and the completion status of each task
After emergency item is added: Writes code or uses reasoning to re-adjust the subsequent task arrangement
After emergency item is added: Uses the delete function to delete plans after 3:30 PM
After emergency item is added: Uses the insert function to submit the modified plan
After emergency item is added: The statistically obtained completion status matches the actual situation
Before emergency item is added: Task D is not completed at the designated time point (3 PM)
Before emergency item is added: Task C is not completed before 5 PM
After emergency item is added: Task Y is not completed before 4:30 PM
After emergency item is added: Task C is not completed before 5 PM
After arrangement: Tasks A, B, C, D, X, and Y are not all completed

title: Quantum Resource Estimation for CH4 Simulation
description:
You are an assistant to a quantum computing expert. You need to understand quantum algorithms, the implementation and programming of quantum circuits, and the performance of different quantum hardware. You will need to use a browser, and you will use code and reasoning. 

We are designing a quantum algorithm to simulate vibrational dynamics, and we need to estimate the quantum resources for the specific task of simulating a CH_4 molecule, which has 9 vibrational modes, over a time evolution of approximately 1.8 ps. For this task, we will represent the Hamiltonian using the Christiansen second quantization formalism. The core objective is to determine the approximate number of Toffoli gates required. Please follow the guidance step-by-step to achieve the goal. Step 1: Design the quantum algorithm for the Hamiltonian simulation. To reduce hardware requirements, we will adopt the Trotterization method. We will set the precision to be roughly 10 cm⁻¹, for the difference of eigenvalues. i Note that the Hamiltonian is represented using Christiansen second quantization. Step 2: Draw the quantum circuit and determine if it can be further optimized. You are encouraged to search the literature. Step 3: Based on the quantum algorithm and circuit, use the PennyLane package (https://docs.pennylane.ai/en/stable/code/api/pennylane.labs.resource_estimation.html#module-pennylane.labs.resource_estimation) to write the quantum circuit in code according to PennyLane's documentation. Run the code to estimate how many Toffoli gates are needed for the CH_4 task. Note that the final answer must be based on the result from running the code. Providing an answer without code that can be run will be considered cheating.

score_criteria.content: Searched online based on the problem description and found the paper "Trotter simulation of vibrational Hamiltonians on a quantum computer"
Successfully found the Trotterization quantum algorithm from the paper
Found the error analysis in the paper and, through simple calculation, reached the conclusion that $10 \text{ cm}^{-1} \approx 1.23984 \times 10^{-3} \text{ eV}$
Found the pennylane package based on the problem description and found the plre.ResourceTrotterVibrational function
Wrote the code by inputting the given conditions and successfully ran it
Noted that the question asks for the Toffoli gate and not other gates (e.g., T gate), and provided the correct answer

title: Training Camp Registration Analysis
description:
1. First, filter by theme to select activities with the theme "TitanData Data & AI Training Camp".
2. Remove supplier test data, i.e., data where company is "Xinda, Xinda Tech, or X-Tech".
3. Separate registration data between CloudEngine employees and non-CloudEngine employees. CloudEngine employees are filtered by company field values of "Yunqing Tech、Qingtian Data、Feiteng Network、Feiteng Network Limited Company, CloudEngine, TitanData".
4. Output CloudEngine's registration information.
5. Perform data analysis on non-CloudEngine registration data: 
  1. Count total number of registrations
  2. Count number of registrations by each registration channel
  3. List companies of registrants and the number of registrants from each company
  4. Analyze registrants' industry/job role/city information, preferably with visualization
6. Based on the above statistical information, provide a brief written summary and suggestions on how to focus future promotional efforts for this event.
7. Format as a message suitable for posting on office platforms like Teams/Feishu, rather than outputting a document or report.

score_criteria.content: Successfully selected TitanData Data and AI Experiment Training Camp activities
Correctly removed supplier data (e.g., data from companies such as Xinda, Xinda Tech, and X-Tech)
Identified duplicate company names (e.g., "Yunqing Tech" and "Yunqing Tech")
TitanData employees and non-TitanData employees are classified accurately, with an error rate of less than 5%
Successfully listed TitanData employee registration information separately and output [TitanData_employee_list.xlsx]
Successfully output the company and frequency of non-TitanData employees separately and generated [TitanData_external_list.xlsx]
All statistical data is calculated correctly with clear logic
Created various types of professional charts (pie charts, bar charts, maps, etc.) to display industry, job role, and city distribution information
IM message format meets specifications, has clear hierarchy, and prominent key information
Missing company name matching/standardization
Topic filtering error, including incorrect activity data or missing target activity
Supplier test data is not completely cleared, affecting the accuracy of analysis results
TitanData employee identification error
Serious problem with duplicate counting or missing counting
Chart cannot be displayed normally or has serious formatting errors
Chart data does not match statistical results
The final output format does not meet the requirements of the IM platform

reference_answer_description:
1. Overall Registration Status
- Total registrations: 57 people
- External client coverage: Estimated 41 client companies, 47 people from external organizations
- TitanData internal participation: 10 people
2. Industry Distribution
- Software & Internet dominates (38.3%, 18 people), aligning with the technical event positioning
- Manufacturing, Gaming, and Financial Services show high participation (10.6% each, 5 people), demonstrating continuous demand for cloud services and digital transformation trends
- Retail, Automotive, Media & Entertainment and other industries also participated, reflecting industry diversification
3. Job Role Distribution
- High proportion of technical decision-makers: Developer / Engineer accounts for 40.4% (19 people), IT Professional or Technical Manager 16 people
- Senior leadership participation: Total of 6 people (12.8%), including VP 2 people, IT Executive 1 person, Business Executive 2 people, CFO / CMO 1 person
4. Geographic Distribution
- Primarily from Beijing: 45 people (95.7%)
- Other cities: Huainan 1 person, Shenzhen 1 person
5. Marketing Channel Performance
- Main channel outstanding performance: Exhibition Poster QR Code contributed 61.7% of registrations (29 people)
- Stable Direct Access ratio: 25.5% (12 people), demonstrating brand influence
- Multi-channel synergy:
  - SMS Link: 2 people
  - Share: 1 person
  - Event QR Code: 1 person
  - Poster QR Code: 1 person
  - Information Query QR Code: 1 person

title: iPhone Upgrade Cost Analysis
description: I currently own an iPhone 13 Pro Max and use AT&T with a $20/month prepaid plan. I want to upgrade to an iPhone 17 Pro Max. Based on the purchase options and carrier deals provided in the attached file, identify the plan or purchase method that gives me the lowest overall cost.

score_criteria.content: Clearly compares the direct purchase plan on Apple's official website with the plans of various operators, and provides a total cost comparison item by item
The calculation process includes the complete logic of device price - trade-in/self-sale subsidy + plan fee
The results include a 36-month total cost model, with 24 months (T-Mobile) explained separately
Clearly indicates the lowest cost plan and explains the reasons (such as plan price difference, contract lock-in period, etc.)
Provides an additional explanation of the freedom and limitations of contract plans versus the official website direct purchase plan
Provides a customized recommendation based on the user's existing conditions (iPhone 13 Pro Max + $20/month plan)
Determines whether the user can get the highest trade-in credit based on the document content
Provides a sensitivity analysis (e.g., changes in results based on trade-in value or plan price fluctuations)
Explains the potential risks and time-sensitive nature of the contract plan (e.g., early termination penalty, loss of benefits upon downgrade, discount validity period)
The presentation of results is intuitive (including comparison tables, charts, or step-by-step explanations that users can understand at a glance)
Fails to incorporate the user's provided conditions (iPhone 13 Pro Max, $20/month plan)
Fails to calculate the total cost of ownership (TCO), only listing unit prices or discounts
Failure to provide a clear conclusion on the best solution
Lack of explanation of contract restrictions or potential risks

title: A-Share Company Research Report
description: I'm trying to conduct company research. I currently have two research reports, but neither covers the company I want to study. Please help me find an A-share listed company whose revenue exceeds RMB 100 billion (based on the 1H/ interim report for 2025) but whose market capitalization is below RMB 100 billion (as of 1 September 2025). Conduct the research following the report format described above (the four sections: company fundamentals, industry landscape, business operations, and DCF valuation & financial forecast) and provide me a PPT.

title: Ice and Snow Character Image Generation and PDF Integration
description:
1.  Analyze the two uploaded images to identify and replicate their overall style. The background must strictly be an ice and snow environment; this is non-negotiable. The ground must be covered with thick, clearly-textured snow, free of any extraneous objects or clutter.

2.  The character's size must be strictly controlled to be smaller, occupying approximately 1/5 of the entire frame.

3.  There must be a sun in the top-right corner of the sky. The sun should be a soft, circular orb of light with a slight halo effect, not overly dazzling. Its position is fixed in the top-right area of the frame.

4.  The clothing must be a down jacket, exactly identical to the one in the example images, including style and color. The character's right hand should be slightly raised at a 45-degree angle, not exceeding chest level.

5.  Remove any "AI-generated" watermarks or text that may have appeared in the original images. Ensure the new images have no such text.

6.  Generate a total of three reference images of the character: one front view, one back view, and one side view.

7.  Strictly adhere to these requirements. Do not include any elements not mentioned. Adding extra details is strictly forbidden.

8.  The final generated images must be in PNG format.

9. Combine the two original images and the three newly generated images into a single PDF file. The PDF must be structured as follows:
    *   Page 1: Start with the title "Original Images," followed by the two original images below it.
    *   Page 2: Start with the title "New Modified Images," followed by the three new images below it.

10. On the second page of the PDF, the three new images must be arranged in this specific order: front view, side view, back view. The PDF file must be named "Ice and Snow Character 1.pdf". The total document must be exactly 2 pages long.

score_criteria.content: The overall image design style is identical to the provided images (e.g., same background, same color scheme)
The ground is covered with thick, textured snow and is free of any clutter, as required
A soft sun with a halo effect is present in the top-right corner of the image
The character's size occupies 1/5 of the entire frame
The PDF is structured correctly with PNG images and the required titles ("Original Images", "New Modified Images")
The generated character has the identical clothing and style
Three images are generated with the required views (front, back, side)
No extra or unmentioned details are added to the images
The PDF is named 'Ice and Snow Character 1.pdf' and the new images are ordered correctly (front, side, back)
The requirement for the character's right hand (raised 45 degrees, not exceeding chest level) is not met
The 'AI-generated' text is not removed from the new images
The total page count of the PDF is not exactly 2 pages

title: Expand Research Lab Presentation
description:
Task: Learn from the attached PPTX and produce an expanded 4-slide version that preserves intent but improves clarity, structure, and visuals.
Audience & tone
Audience: faculty hiring/oversight committees and prospective students/postdocs.
Tone: concise, professional, forward-looking; avoid marketing fluff.
Language
English only.
Use short sentences and scannable bullets (≤12 words per bullet).
No jargon unless essential; define once if used.
Slide count & required content (exactly 4 slides)
Lab Setup Phase (Year 1) — “four goals” section
Derive 4 concrete goals from the source deck.
If the source implies fewer than 4, infer additional goals consistent with the plan (e.g., platform readiness, initial data/preprint, onboarding/safety SOPs, basic automation).
Each goal = 1 line + a 1-line success metric (e.g., “instrument platform online; ≥1 validated dataset; 1 preprint draft”).
Rolling Expansion (Years 2–5) — bullets
Summarize intake cadence, team caps, and mentoring structure as bullets.
Include constraints (e.g., ≤6 Ph.D. students; joint-supervised students don’t count toward cap; 1–2 postdocs; 2–4 rotating UROPs).
Add 1 bullet on dynamic resourcing (projects/funding dependent).
Recruitment Preferences & Rationale
Bullets that explain why Ph.D. students are prioritized (plasticity, medium/long-term projects, team renewal).
Bullet on early postdoc staffing (“accelerate platform; achieve representative results; Xiaodong-group experience”).
Bullet on joint-training policy (not counted toward cap).
Stage Milestones
Y1, Y2–Y3, Y4–Y5 as three rows (or stacked bullets).
Keep to outcomes only (platform online; first data/preprints; stable publications; expand lines/instrument capability; talent pipeline; landmark results; global visibility).
Design & formatting constraints
Light theme. Clean, modern layout; strong hierarchy.
Title ≤70 chars; slide text ≤6 bullets per slide; ≤40 chars per bullet when possible.
Use tasteful supporting imagery (abstract/stock textures only). No charts/tables with text (unless you have a real figure).
If an image has overlaid text, apply a subtle fade to ensure readability.
Icons: at most 1–2 per slide; none on Slides 1–2 titles.
No leftover template placeholders or “lorem ipsum.”
Consistent typography across slides; align to grid; adequate white space.
Content rules
Strictly base facts on the source deck; do not invent headcount beyond what’s given.
You may infer missing but obvious operational goals (e.g., SOPs, onboarding) for Slide 1—mark inferred items with “(inferred)” in the speaker notes, not on the slide.
Maintain the policy that jointly supervised Ph.D. students are excluded from the cap.
Deliverables
A 4-slide PPTX.
Speaker notes (≤60 words per slide) clarifying any inferences or terminology.

score_criteria.content: Accurately learn from the original PPT and distill the key points correctly.
Produce exactly 4 slides, strictly corresponding to the four required titles.
Each slide contains ≤6 bullet points, and each bullet contains ≤12 English words.
Correctly state key policies, such as joint training not counting toward the doctoral student quota.
Provide ≤60 words of speaker notes for each slide.
Font sizes are set appropriately, resulting in well-balanced white space.
Not strictly limited to 4 slides or slide titles do not match the requirements.
Inconsistencies with key facts from the original content (e.g., numbers, quotas, policies).
Addition of new factual content without proper indication or citation.
Phase milestones do not cover Y1, Y2–Y3, and Y4–Y5.
Presence of unnecessary images or flashy effects that hinder effective information delivery.

title: Cookbook Development and Marketing
description: I want to build a cookbook for home chefs. Start by analyzing the top 100 best-selling cookbooks of the last decade for popular themes and recipes. Then, research cooking blogs and YouTube channels with the highest engagement to identify trending topics. Based on the findings, draft a unique, themed cookbook concept. Develop a sample chapter and design mockups for the book. Then, create a free content strategy to market the cookbook (e.g., recipes online, Instagram reels). Finally, prepare a launch plan to self-publish and market it.

score_criteria.content: Provide a comprehensive list of the top 100 best-selling cookbooks of the last decade with accurate details (e.g., themes, popular recipes).
Identify high-engagement cooking blogs and YouTube channels, and extract trending topics related to cookbook themes and recipes.
Deliver a precise and actionable summary of success factors and patterns extracted from the analyzed cookbooks, blogs, and YouTube channels.
Propose a creative and practical themed cookbook concept based on the analysis.
Develop a sample chapter with clear content, including recipes, instructions, and visuals that align with the proposed concept.
Design mockups of the cookbook that are visually appealing, aligned with the theme, and professional in presentation.
Include creative, cost-effective marketing ideas in the free content strategy document (e.g., Instagram reels, recipe blogs, influencer partnerships).
Ensure a well-documented launch plan for self-publishing, including operational, marketing, and customer acquisition strategies.
Cookbook mockups are poorly designed or do not align with the theme and professional standards.

title: Update CPEFX Investment Schedule
description: Using the attached 'Cascade Private Capital Fund (CPEFX) – Schedule of Investments', I need to prepare a version for our quarterly review meeting. Please perform the following modifications: first, identify all investments with an 'initial acquisition date' in the year 2025. For each of these recent investments, add an asterisk (*) to the end of their name. Second, add a footnote at the very bottom of the document that reads: '* Indicates an investment acquired in 2025.' Finally, save the updated schedule as a new HTML file named 'CPEFX_Schedule_with_highlights.html'.
attachment_filenames: taskif_159 + attachment + Cascade Private Capital Fund (CPEFX) – Schedule of Investments (June 30 2025).pdf

score_criteria.content: Correctly appends an asterisk (*) to the name of every identified 2025 investment.
Adds the specified footnote with the exact text '* Indicates an investment acquired in 2025.' and places the footnote at the logical bottom of the document content, after the main investment table.
Fails to save the output file in a HTML format with the exact name 'CPEFX_Schedule_with_highlights.html'.
Adds asterisks to investments whose 'initial acquisition date' is not in 2025.
Modifies any data that was not supposed to be changed (e.g., dates, costs, fair values, non-target names).

title: CTA Best Practices Guide
description: Your task is to create an interactive, single-page best-practices guide for web Call-to-Action (CTA) buttons, using the provided PDF documents as source material. Start with a basic `guide.html`. First, add a new section titled 'The Purpose of a CTA' and populate it with key points from HubSpot's guide (`LT_eBook_CreateEffectiveCTAs_v2.pdf`). Next, refine the existing 'CTA Design Principles' section by replacing its placeholder content with the specific do's and don'ts from the LSE guide (`How-to-create-and-use-CTA-buttons.pdf`), including the distinction between primary (red) and secondary (white) buttons. Then, make the guide navigable by adding unique `id` attributes to all `<h2>` section headers and creating a `<nav>` bar at the top with anchor links to each section. Create a new `styles.css` file and add rules to make the navigation bar fixed at the top with a clean background color. Crucially, you must also add the CSS classes `.cta-primary` and `.cta-secondary` as defined in the LSE guide. To make the guide interactive, create a `script.js` file that adds a 'Toggle Style' button to a demo CTA; clicking this button should toggle the button's class between `.cta-primary` and `.cta-secondary`. Ensure you properly link both the CSS and JS files in `guide.html`. Finally, package the completed `guide.html`, `styles.css`, and `script.js` files into a single zip archive named `cta_best_practices_guide.zip`.

score_criteria.content: Populates the 'The Purpose of a CTA' section with relevant key points "drive traffic to a landing page" extracted from the HubSpot PDF (`LT_eBook_CreateEffectiveCTAs_v2.pdf`).
Replaces placeholder content in the 'CTA Design Principles' section with specific do's and don'ts from the LSE guide (`How-to-create-and-use-CTA-buttons.pdf`).
Correctly includes the distinction between primary (red) and secondary (white) buttons in the guide's content, as described in the LSE guide.
Adds unique `id` attributes to all `<h2>` section headers and creates a functional `<nav>` bar with anchor links to each section.
Creates `styles.css` and correctly styles the navigation bar to be fixed at the top of the viewport with a background color.
Defines the `.cta-primary` and `.cta-secondary` CSS classes in `styles.css` with styles (e.g., colors, padding) that accurately match the specifications from the LSE guide.
Creates `script.js` and correctly implements the JavaScript logic to toggle a demo button's CSS class between `.cta-primary` and `.cta-secondary` on click.
The final output archive is not named `cta_best_practices_guide.zip` or the zip archive is missing one or more of the required files (`guide.html`, `styles.css`, `script.js`).
The JavaScript functionality is broken, throws console errors, or does not correctly toggle the classes.
CSS classes for the CTA buttons are named incorrectly (e.g., `.primary-button` instead of `.cta-primary`).

title: Tesla Sustainability Data Analysis
description: You are tasked with conducting an analysis of Tesla's sustainability data. Follow these steps: 1. Visit Tesla's 'Impact Report' page (link: https://www.tesla.com/impact), and verify access to the 2024 and 2022 reports. 2. Open the reports and extract data from specific pages in both years – find information on renewable energy used, water usage, and carbon emissions. 3. Create an Excel file named Tesla.csv where the first row includes the year, the first column includes the type of data, and fill in the extracted data. 4. Calculate whether water usage decreased in 2024. If so, calculate the year-over-year percentage improvement in water usage per vehicle; otherwise, calculate the year-over-year reduction in carbon emissions. Document calculations in Excel. 5. Generate a bar chart visualizing the year-over-year trends from Step 4. Save as [tesla_sustainability.jpg].

score_criteria.content: Extracts the data for 2.57 m^3/vehicle water usage, and 13.4 million/carbon emissions from specified pages for 2022 reports
Extracts the data for 2.8 PWh renewable energy used, 0.45 m^3/vehicle water usage, and 32 million/carbon emissions from specified pages for 2024 reports
switches water usage to year-over-year decrease from 2022-2024
Generates a clear bar chart that visualizes water usage trends calculated in Step 4 [tesla_sustainability.jpg]
Fails to verify access to both 2024 and 2022 reports or uses an incorrect URL
Excel file is not named Tesla.csv or formatted as specified (e.g., first row is not year, or first column misses some data)
Chart file does not meet format or naming requirements (e.g., incorrect file type or name)

title: Cat Food Comparison Analysis
description:
You are an AI assistant being evaluated for your ability to perform multi-step, web-based research and build a useful, honest product comparison webpage.
I regularly buy cat food and want a balanced, up-to-date overview of popular options across major online retailers. I care not only about high ratings, but also about problems, complaints, and any safety concerns.

Your task
Create a single-page HTML website that compares best-selling cat foods from:
  Amazon,  Petco, Chewy,  Walmart
 
 You must follow the workflow:
1) Collect products: For each of the four websites, identify 3 best-selling cat foods (focus on general cat food, dry or wet; avoid treats and very narrow medical diets unless they clearly dominate the bestseller list). If the same product appears on multiple sites, you should detect and merge it as the same product, while still showing which retailers carry it.
2) For each product, collect:
Product name and brand 
 Retailer(s) where it was found
 Approximate price and size (e.g. “$23.99 for 7 lb bag”)
 Star rating and number of reviews on each retailer where possible
3) Then search broadly on the web (beyond the retailer page) for additional information such as:
Independent reviews or blog posts
Vet or expert commentary (if available)
4) Surface both positive and negative information: 
Do not just report “4.7 stars, great!”
For each product, you must explicitly include:
Pros (e.g. cats like it, good ingredients, specific benefits)
Cons / “bad news” such as: Common complaints (e.g. vomiting, diarrhea, picky eaters), Known or suspected formula changes that upset customers, Any recall history, safety alerts, or serious criticism, if found
5)Build the HTML webpage
The final output should be a single, self-contained HTML document that could be saved as cat_food_comparison.html and opened directly in a browser.
It should include:
 -A clear title and short introduction explaining what the page is about
 -A summary section (overall observations, patterns, warnings)
 -A comparison layout for the products, for example:
-Sections grouped by product (preferred), showing which retailers carry it or sections grouped by retailer, clearly labeling duplicates
 
  For each product:
   -Name and brand
 
  -Retailers and price/size info
 
  -Overall rating overview (per retailer if available)
 
  -A short Pros list (2–5 bullet points)
 
  -A short Cons / Concerns list (2–5 bullet points)
 
  -A brief text summary (2–4 sentences) combining the findings
 
  Include simple CSS styling (inline <style> is fine) so the page is easy to read.
 
  Add a “Sources” section at the bottom listing the key pages you consulted (retailer links, review sites, any recall notices, etc.).

score_criteria.content: The model identifies when the same product appears on multiple sites and merges the information, showing clearly that it is sold across several retailers (with per-retailer prices/ratings).
Meaningful negative signals (“bad news”)Cons/concerns are non-trivial and clearly informed by actual complaints, issues, or recall/safety info (not generic “some cats might not like it”).

Good synthesis, not just copy-paste. The summary text per product and the overall summary section show real synthesis (e.g. “this food is generally liked but has many complaints about hairballs” or “great for sensitive stomachs but expensive”), not just restating ratings.
Explicitly calls out limitations / caveats. The page acknowledges that cats differ, that online reviews can be biased, and that owners should consult vets for medical issues.
Missing retailer. One or more of Amazon, Petco, Chewy, Walmart is not represented or is clearly under-represented (e.g. only 1–2 products instead of 5).
Only positive spin. The model lists star ratings and pros but provides almost no meaningful negative information, despite the prompt explicitly requiring “bad news”.
No sources listed. The page does not contain any explicit links or source mentions, even though web search was required.
